speeches.name: Jerome H. Powell
speeches.summary: Powell emphasized that the Fed is still balancing downside labor-market risks against upside inflation risks while keeping policy appropriately restrictive and not committing to near-term cuts.

speeches.name: Michael S. Barr
speeches.summary: Barr highlighted uncertainty in the outlook and kept focus on incoming data, consistent with a cautious wait-and-see stance.

speeches.name: Philip N. Jefferson
speeches.summary: Jefferson stressed data dependence and the need to ensure inflation continues moving toward target before easing policy.

speeches.name: Michelle W. Bowman
speeches.summary: Bowman leaned more hawkish by emphasizing inflation persistence and the risk of easing too soon.

speeches.name: Lorie K. Logan
speeches.role: President, Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas
speeches.summary: Logan underscored the need to keep policy restrictive until inflation is clearly and durably on track to 2%.

macro_fred.narrative: Leaning Neutral: CPI at 4.0%, Fed Funds at 3.62%, unemployment at 4.3%.
